title: History Activity log to be moved when an Item is moved to another board

posts.author: johana.Echelon (Johanna Izquierdo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Good afternoon,

When an item is moved to another board the History of activity log get lost. Is it possible for the activity log to be moved as part of the whole item migrated.

We moved several items between boards but are losing the activity log every-time the item is moved.

Thank you,

posts.author: bogdandobrin (Bogdan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Blockquote When an item is moved to another board the History of activity log get lost. Is it possible for the activity log to be moved as part of the whole item migrated.

We moved several items between boards but are losing the activity log every-time the item is moved.

Hi there - someone in my team is also interested in this feature. Are there any updates on it?

posts.author: Christophe (SC)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

I support this request.
Indeed, with the organization I have defined between my teams, each have there own board, and in addition, there is one common. I have defined several automates to move entries from the main board (the common one) to the team board depending on statuses…
I really miss the history log once the items have been moved: some important information are lost.

So please, either add a new automate to generate a file attached in an update of the entry with the copy of the history log, or better, just keep this history log within the entry, even after it has been moved.

Thanks

posts.author: evalumo (Louis-Philippe Tremblay)
posts.cookedHtml:
Huge problem for us. Trying to keep track of the origin of a Lead to know how much to pay a sales rep (Cold Lead vs Warm Lead).

Please consider this

posts.author: e_dutton (Elizabeth Dutton)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would also love to see this feature. Our team has to “archive” items to a seperate board at the end of the month and we will lose details regarding automations and other actions.
OR
Is there widget that can maintain that information?

Thanks for considering this feature!

posts.author: swosh (Petr Hnilica)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be great to have it. We move items between 7 broads in production process.

It is posibble to save it using webhook intregation and saving logs through board logs activity:

{
  boards(ids: [457232545]) {
    id
    items(ids: 3491190462) {
      state
      id
      column_values(ids: "date") {
        text
        title
      }
    }
    activity_logs(item_ids: 3491190462) {
      entity
      data
    }
  }
}

posts.author: rosswsnyder (Ross Snyder)
posts.cookedHtml: Our company would love to have this feature built in. We move tasks from board to board every day. All of the notes are moved along with the task, but we also need the activity history to move. It would be nice to have this built in without any custom/manual webhook or API integrations. Maybe make it an option for a company to choose? Not having the activity history has been difficult to go back and follow-up on a task later when we need to know when a status changed or other changes were made and who made the changes.

posts.author: Tonebar (Tony B)
posts.cookedHtml: I am too encountering this problem. Even the updates are lost when moving items between boards. Not good from the audit/traceability/accountability point of views.

posts.author: EyalSeagull (Eyal Seagull)
posts.cookedHtml: We need this feature, too. The item is the same one, even when moved between boards, so activity log should be connected to the item, no matter in which board it is located in the moment.

posts.author: MatthewB (Matthew Buchmeier)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a really big problem for me as well. I have processes subject to audit and I lose all history when moving items between boards. My workflows require movement between boards in order to manage access and authority. I can’t say that in status “A” these specific users can modify data but when status changes to “B” then only a different set of users can modify data on the board or within a column. My only partial solution is to run an automation before the item is moved to the next board that writes an update to the item. Within the update I have to attempt to log all of the data for that item, who approved or changed the status on the item, etc. and then let the automation move it to the next board. There are very few features I would rank higher than this one.

posts.author: jthorn (Josiah Thorngate)
posts.cookedHtml: Chiming in on this. It’s hugely annoying that activity from the previous board disappears from the log on the current board—if I move the item back, the old activity reappears, so the data is still there. Why does it need to disappear? Please fix it.

posts.author: nataliav (Natalia V)
posts.cookedHtml: Our company processes suffer a lot from losing history, too. We use boards to build a workflow and it’s crucial we keep the history.

posts.author: KMcG (Kelly McGarity)
posts.cookedHtml: We definitely could use the history in the activity log when moving items from one board to another. This seems like a function that should already be implemented.

posts.author: ilg-comex (Iluska)
posts.cookedHtml: We have the same issue, since monday is now limiting the numer of linked connect item to 10k per board I need to create a new board and move a huge sum of items to this board and losing a lot of needed information on the activity log

posts.author: greenersolutions (Kyla Kryski)
posts.cookedHtml: Add us to the list of companies that have a huge issue with losing the activity log! We lost a ton of historical data when we had to move items to new boards and it’s come up several times when management has asked when certain statuses changed and all of that activity data is now lost.

posts.author: jailynnhoops (Jai-Lynn Hoops)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is an issue for us as well.

Overall this feature is something that should be really considered because for many companies like my own this is a track record/easy audit when clients or internally we have questions about our process.

posts.author: BennLM (Benn Moffat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any chance there is an update coming from Monday.com on this issue, it’s a massive waste of time, to go back to the Leads board to find activity history once a lead is moved to the quotes board for us!
Anything to tell us Monday.com?

posts.author: uprsgroup (Aaron)
posts.cookedHtml: This is very important for us!

posts.author: Cnichols (C N)
posts.cookedHtml: For any company using SOC audits this is a huge deal. We need the history of an item to stay with that item no matter what boards it gets moved to. It is surprising to me that this isn’t just standard function in the product.

posts.author: ap001 (Alexi Paasonen)
posts.cookedHtml: not ideal, but a workaround seems to be to re-add it to the original board, and the activity log seems to reappear

posts.author: Cnichols (C N)
posts.cookedHtml: But then we lose the history from the other board that item had spent time on. I can’t believe this hasn’t been added to the list of items to work on. The history is still there, since it shows back up when you move the item back to that board, so why can’t it display in the history of the item no matter where that item moves to?

posts.author: Engin (Engin Yeke)
posts.cookedHtml: Isn’t there still an update on this? It is a must for us as well.

posts.author: ahrabovsky (ahrabovsky)
posts.cookedHtml: We’re also looking for this feature! Can we please get an update?

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: How does your automation to create an update with the log work?

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am also still in need of this functionality. Please keep the activity log attached to the item, when moving between boards.
We clutter our updates with changes and unnecessarily use up our automations as a workaround and noone is happy with that.

posts.author: MatthewB (Matthew Buchmeier)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Nils, I used an automation in a workflow where an item was being submitted for approval. It was formatted similar to "When STATUS changes to SOMETHING move ITEM to BOARD and create an update with THIS TEXT’ and then I selected the existing data elements so at least at the point of transfer I could retain what values the person had entered originally even after the item moved through multiple boards during processing. Best I could come up and was only useable under very limited conditions.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for the explanation!

So to clarify:
You could only “save” the latest state just prior to moving and not all the changes from creation to moving?

posts.author: MatthewB (Matthew Buchmeier)
posts.cookedHtml:
Correct. This automation only serves to capture current values at the time the item is moving to the new board so it’s usefulness is limited.

posts.author: Logan_INSIDE (Logan)
posts.cookedHtml: I Agree, it’s a huge problem.

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the option - unfortunately it doesn’t have the same historical information required. i.e. “Who” did “what” for tracking purposes, “what” automated triggered this action. This is critical information needed for an item. If duplicating an item can copy over the updates, why can’t moving an item retain the historical information?

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml: How do you mean reappear? What work around are you doing?

posts.author: djohnston (Dawn Johnston)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! Adding in my request for this to be addressed please! I’m currently trying to justify a timeline to my leadership team and the activity log is GONE because the task moved to another board. Please move this up the priority list! Thank you!!!

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: This should not be the solution in terms of this request but just for your case: If you move the item back to its original board, you will be able to access the activity log again.

posts.author: sansizone (Iron)
posts.cookedHtml: I can’t believe this issue was raised back in 2020 and there’s still no response

posts.author: ivan_b (Ivan)
posts.cookedHtml:
If that helps anyone on the topic - apparently activity log is retained in this case it’s just that it doesn’t show up on UI.
If you need to access it - you may try to use a GraphQL query and access via API (and potentially some 3d party tools to process data in bulk if needed).
API documentation related: Activity logs

Sample GraphQL query - update if needed:
query {

boards (ids: [original_board_id, moved_to_board_id]) {

activity_logs (from: "2025-10-06T00:00:00Z", to: "2025-10-09T00:00:00Z", item_ids: \[if_needed_specific_item_ids\], column_ids: \["column1", "column2"\]) {

  id

  event

  data

  user_id

}

}

}

posts.author: kadfire (Adam Williams)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this feature. We use Monday for auditing dispatches and when we move items over to other boards we lose any info relating to dispatch dates etc.

posts.author: lmccoig (Lindsey)
posts.cookedHtml: Would also love to see this feature added.

posts.author: SMCarter (Sarah Carter)
posts.cookedHtml: Another organization who would benefit from this chiming in. At current state, to comply with audit requirements, I have to create monstrous boards with process stages as groups for my internal clients instead of being able to move their items seamlessly through different process stage boards. Please consider this feature.

posts.author: chase (Chase Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml: New user in first month of Monday - It is crazy this has not been implemented yet from 2020 request?

posts.author: janeo (Jane O'Brien)
posts.cookedHtml: We too face this issue. It would be very beneficial if Monday.com considered this as a new feature!

posts.author: Abi.Labs (Abi)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree, this is also an essential for us > I expect the Activity Log to retain all activities related to that item

posts.author: well1985 (Valentine)
posts.cookedHtml: Needed for us as well , please consider to add this feature if possible
